response.justification: While Szabo demonstrates exceptional technical depth across 25+ years of full-stack development and some infrastructure experience at Altom, he is significantly underqualified for this people-leadership role. The position explicitly requires 2 years of team management experience, and the resume shows no evidence of people management, performance reviews, team development, or organizational change leadership—only technical individual-contributor and staff-level roles. His generalist background also doesn't align with the specialized focus on developer tools, build systems, and testing infrastructure optimization.
strengths: 25+ years of software development spanning web, mobile, desktop, embedded systems, and backend services with demonstrated technical mastery
Infrastructure and DevOps expertise: established CI/CD pipelines, GitLab instances, Docker deployments, and dev tooling at Altom; currently contributing to MCP frameworks
Technical leadership culture: organized Refactoring Dojos and Selenium training, created TDD workshops, mentored others in software craftsmanship practices
concerns: No demonstrated people management experience: resume contains no evidence of managing teams, conducting 1-on-1s, performance reviews, hiring, or people development—critical for a full-time people-leadership role

Limited specialization in developer productivity tools and build systems: primarily a generalist full-stack engineer rather than focused on testing infrastructure, build optimization, or developer experience
No evidence of organizational-level change leadership or strategic roadmapping; experience is primarily hands-on technical contribution rather than setting team priorities and vision
